name: engram
description: Cognitive memory system for coding agents with hybrid retrieval, memory lifecycle management, MCP tools, and a local web dashboard.
approachDetail: Combines HNSW dense retrieval, BM25/FTS keyword search, entity graph traversal, Hopfield associative retrieval, RRF fusion, reranking, and lifecycle scoring.
searchDetail: Hybrid search uses dense embeddings, BM25 full-text search, graph traversal, Hopfield retrieval, intent-weighted RRF, cross-encoder reranking, and optional learned reranking.
storageDetail: Local installs use a single SQLite database; service deployments can migrate to PostgreSQL for concurrent web and MCP clients.
mcpDetail: Provides a JSON-RPC MCP server over stdio plus an HTTP/SSE transport.
toolSupportDetail: Docs include Claude Code, Codex, generic stdio MCP, and HTTP/SSE client configuration snippets.
scopesDetail: Supports session handoffs and diaries, project/codebase scan memories, and a shared global memory database.
duplicateDetail: Uses embedding-distance surprise scoring at write time and semantic deduplication with configurable similarity thresholds.
retentionDetail: Working and episodic memories can promote by importance/access count; low-retention memories decay or archive while semantic, procedural, and pinned memories persist.
multiUserDetail: Multiple agents can share the same database, but there is no built-in user/account isolation model.
crossMachineDetail: Can run as a central web/MCP service backed by PostgreSQL, but does not provide a hosted sync layer.
webUiDetail: Local dashboard includes neural map, search, memory browsing, entities, analytics, retention, deduplication, drift, events, and export views.
tokenBudgetingDetail: MCP tools include top_k retrieval and max_tokens context/compression helpers.
contextInjectionDetail: Agents call MCP tools such as recall_context, layers, resume_context, and get_skills; session handoffs refresh automatically inside the MCP server.
networkDetail: Local embeddings and reranking can run offline after model download; optional OpenAI, Voyage, Gemini, and Anthropic backends require network/API keys.
auditTrailDetail: Status transitions are stored in status_history, with event logs, access logs, diary entries, session handoffs, annotations, and importance history.
pros: Strong hybrid retrieval stack beyond plain vector search
Memory lifecycle, deduplication, and retention controls
Rich local dashboard for inspection and maintenance
SQLite default with PostgreSQL path for concurrent deployments
cons: No built-in hosted sync or account-based multi-user isolation
Context injection depends on agent/tool usage rather than being universally automatic
Advanced enrichment and consolidation may require local model setup or external API keys
